aliases: Korean seaweed rice roll with chicken

english: Korean seaweed rice roll with chicken

ontology.foodex2_description: Rice-based composite dish — seaweed rice roll (gimbap/kimbap), chicken variant; FoodEx2 category for composite cereal-based dishes with meat filling
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: No FoodEx2 code exists specifically for chicken gimbap; A0FKP (composite cereal-based dish) used as the closest defensible category anchor. Chicken variant noted in description.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for gimbap or Korean seaweed rice rolls as of 2026.

serving.natural_language_portion: A standard serving of chicken gimbap is one full roll (approximately 200 g), typically cut into 6–8 bite-sized pieces and consumed as a complete meal or substantial snack.

nutrient_source.title: Korean Gimbap (Chicken) — HPB Singapore Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Korean Gimbap (Chicken), CR ID F05100068. All core macros and secondary nutrients are taken from this record with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat not reported by HPB; estimated at 0.8 g/100 g based on USDA FoodData Central entry for chicken and rice composite dishes (USDA SR Legacy #21420 and #20444 blend) as a supporting reference.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC; and University of Sydney GI Database entry for sushi/rice rolls
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal (peer-reviewed)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Korean chicken gimbap in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI estimated at 48 (low) based on the closest approved analogous food: Japanese sushi rice rolls (GI ~48–55, University of Sydney GI Database). Gimbap shares the same structural composition — vinegar/sesame-seasoned short-grain white rice wrapped in seaweed — which moderates the GI of plain white rice (GI ~72) through the acidity of seasoning, seaweed wrapping, and mixed fillings. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (48 × 36.0) / 100 = 17.3 for a 200 g serving (36 g available carbohydrate).

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 463 mg/100 g with insufficient potassium offset
hypertension.guidance: At 463 mg sodium per 100 g, a standard 200 g serving delivers approximately 926 mg sodium — nearly 40% of the 2,400 mg daily limit — making this dish unsuitable for hypertensive individuals without sodium-reduction modifications such as requesting less pickled radish and reduced salt in rice seasoning.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Refined white rice carbohydrate load (18 g/100 g) with low dietary fibre (1.6 g/100 g)
type2_diabetes.guidance: The estimated GI of 48 (low, analogous to sushi rice rolls) and a glycemic load of 17.3 per 200 g serving place this dish in the moderate concern category for type 2 diabetes; portion control to 100–150 g, pairing with high-fibre vegetable sides, and requesting brown rice gimbap where available are recommended strategies.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated sodium (463 mg/100 g) and moderate fat (3.31 g/100 g); low saturated fat profile
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The high sodium content (463 mg/100 g) is the primary cardiovascular concern, as chronic high sodium intake elevates blood pressure and increases cardiovascular risk; the relatively low total fat (3.31 g/100 g) and lean chicken protein are positive attributes, but sodium reduction through preparation modifications is strongly advised for individuals with cardiovascular disease.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Sodium 463 mg/100 g exceeds 400 mg/100 g RA threshold; high glycaemic load promotes inflammation
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Sodium at 463 mg/100 g exceeds the 400 mg/100 g threshold for rheumatoid arthritis, worsening tissue swelling; request reduced salt in preparation, omit or reduce pickled radish (danmuji), and retain spinach and carrot fillings for their antioxidant contribution.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Korean Gimbap (Chicken) — also romanized as Dakgogi Gimbap (닭고기 김밥) — is a Korean seaweed rice roll filled with seasoned chicken and vegetables. It is structurally similar to Japanese sushi rolls but is not made with vinegared rice; instead, short-grain white rice is seasoned with sesame oil and salt, then spread on a sheet of dried seaweed (gim/nori), layered with stir-fried or grilled chicken strips, pickled yellow radish (danmuji), blanched spinach, julienned carrots, and sometimes fish cake or egg, then tightly rolled and sliced into bite-sized rounds. The result is a compact, portable, mildly savoury roll with a chewy rice texture, subtle sesame aroma, and a balance of salty, slightly sweet, and umami flavours from the fillings. Gimbap is a staple Korean street food and packed lunch (dosirak) item, widely available in Korean convenience stores, food courts, and restaurants. Per 100 g, it provides 128 kcal, 6.6 g protein, 3.31 g fat, 18 g carbohydrate, 1.6 g dietary fibre, and 463 mg sodium. The high sodium content (463 mg/100 g) is the primary clinical concern, making it unsuitable for hypertensive and rheumatoid arthritis patients without modification. The estimated GI of 48 (low, analogous to sushi rice rolls) and moderate glycaemic load of 17.3 per 200 g serving make it a moderate concern for type 2 diabetes management. The lean chicken filling provides a useful protein contribution. Calcium (22 mg/100 g) and iron (0.76 mg/100 g) are present at modest levels from the seaweed and vegetable components.

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (CR ID F05100068) with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat estimated from USDA SR Legacy composite dish data as a supporting reference. GI estimated from the closest approved analogous food (sushi rice rolls, University of Sydney GI Database) with clear documentation. All values are internally consistent and plausible for this food type.
notes: 1. Saturated fat (0.8 g/100 g) is estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for cooked white rice and lean chicken composite dishes, as HPB does not report saturated fat for this item. This is an approximation and should be replaced with a direct measurement when available. 2. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, micronutrient panel, and moisture data for this specific Korean chicken gimbap preparation are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026. 3. Potassium (175 mg/100 g) is an estimate derived from USDA SR Legacy component data for white rice, chicken, and vegetable fillings in proportions consistent with gimbap composition; HPB does not report potassium for this item. 4. The dietary_tags in the input JSON incorrectly listed 'vegetarian' and 'plant_based' for a chicken-containing dish; these have been corrected to false in dietary_flags. 5. GI value of 48 is an analogous estimate based on sushi/rice rolls from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for Korean chicken gimbap was found in any approved source. 6. FoodEx2 code A0FKP is used as the closest defensible category anchor for composite cereal-based dishes; no specific FoodEx2 code for gimbap exists in the published FoodEx2 catalogue as of 2026. 7. FoodOn URI http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/FOODON_03302050 is cited as the closest defensible FoodOn match for gimbap as a food product; reviewers should verify this URI against the current FoodOn release.

improvement_actions_taken: Corrected dietary_flags: removed incorrect 'vegetarian' and 'plant_based' tags from input; set is_vegetarian and is_vegan to false for chicken-containing dish.
Added Korean local name 닭고기 김밥 and romanized form Dakgogi Gimbap to satisfy G6 multilingual requirement for Korean cuisine.
Estimated saturated_fat_g (0.8) from USDA SR Legacy composite dish data with full documentation, as HPB does not report this field.
Replaced input GI value of 0 (unsupported) with analogous estimate of 48 from University of Sydney GI Database (sushi rice rolls), with full source documentation.
Added potassium_mg (175) as an estimate from USDA SR Legacy component data with documentation.
Documented all -1 sentinel fields in notes per G3 requirement.
Corrected health_context to use 'type2_diabetes' machine key with 'Type 2 Diabetes' human_condition_name per Q7 requirement.
Added cardiovascular_disease health context (missing from input) to satisfy Q7.
Built comprehensive aliases and aliases_by_language with self-check verification for Q8.
Populated full ontology section with honest match types and gap notes for G5/Q5.
Corrected ontology.foodex2_match_type from 'analogous_estimate' to 'closest_parent' to align with allowed values for G5.
